page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. Single-core CPU performance is consistently low, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks also register in the middle 50%. Notably, the hardware performs strongly in cryptographic operations, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

The instance presents a clear tradeoff between single-threaded execution speed and resource density. While single-core throughput is weak, the high memory capacity and integrated 1800 GB NVMe SSD local storage offer qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large in-memory datasets and fast local scratch space. It avoids the higher costs of newer generation architectures while delivering stable multi-threaded and database performance, as evidenced by average ratings in Redis SET operations and static web serving.

This instance configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications that do not demand high single-core clock speeds. Appropriate workloads include medium-sized databases, in-memory caches like Redis, distributed data processing, and local storage-dependent batch processing. It is less suitable for large language model inference or single-threaded compute tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.2 GHz. It is configured with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 10 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks indicate weak single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the bottom 10%, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance. Cryptographic execution is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25% due to hardware-accelerated instructions. This instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy and local-storage-dependent workloads, making it suitable for database deployments, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing rather than compute-bound or single-threaded applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance combining high-capacity DDR4 memory with local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive and cryptographic workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage

Suited for databases, in-memory caching, and multi-threaded data processing
